name: Ben Carson
subject: They said it was impossible
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Carson America, Inc
body:
---
|
|
---

Peter, we've had a very strong August. A lot of very good things are happening.

We had a great debate in Cleveland, where I had the opportunity to share my values and solutions directly with the American people -- many of whom had never heard of me.

We're firmly in the top tier of nearly every poll, and even leading Hillary Clinton in a number of swing states.

Hundreds of thousands of new supporters and volunteers have signed up, as well as 700,000 new fans on Facebook.

Through my visits to places like Harlem and the U.S.-Mexico border, we've been leading the national conversation on topics like immigration and the need to embrace faith, personal responsibility and conservative reforms in our inner cities.

This is the kind of momentum that the Washington political class said was impossible. But we're going to continue to prove them wrong.

As August comes to a close, more people will begin to tune into this race -- so it's imperative that we finish the month in the strongest way possible.

[**With that in mind, I ask you to make a donation to my campaign before the clock runs out.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/30038934:28808455777:m:1:876315805:A6D29BAE75C5787B596EEAD0D5004F65:r)

I am continually humbled by the number of people I meet on the campaign trail who tell me they've been sending in small grassroots donations of $5, $10 or even $25. That's the strength of this campaign, and it's why we're going to win.

[**So please, help us close out the month in a strong way. Make a donation before the clock runs out.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/30038934:28808455777:m:1:876315805:A6D29BAE75C5787B596EEAD0D5004F65:r)

Thank you for your faith in me, and for your commitment to reviving the nation we love.

-Ben

**Saturday, August 29, 2015**

This week Senator Paul made campaign stops throughout the northwestern United States, from Alaska to Utah. Each event had large, enthusiastic crowds who were fired up about his pro-liberty message. Whether it's cities in Washington, Iowa, or New Hampshire, it's clear that Rand Paul has the broad appeal to run a 50-state operation that can take on the Clinton/Obama machine.

As the Washington Post [noted](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/30058151:28809734773:m:1:879035888:1B532A49DEC55C01466E3B2B83705BDA:r), this is all part of a long-term strategy to pick up support from delegates who other candidates are disregarding. Senator Paul is running to disrupt the status quo, and this is the latest example of that. Stay tuned for next week's Rand Paul for President Newsletter.

Doug Stafford
Chief Strategist
Rand Paul for President
�
�
| | |
## News of the Week
---
| | |
### Rand Paul Vows to Continue Eye Surgery if President
Republican presidential candidate Rand Paul will continue performing eye surgery if elected president, he told CNN on Friday. "You think politics could be frustrating sometimes, you're absolutely right," he said. "But in medicine the amazing thing is we all unify around a goal, someone is blind, we remove the cataract and they can see again. There's probably nothing more rewarding than seeing that smile."

[ ](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/30058152:28809734773:m:1:879035888:1B532A49DEC55C01466E3B2B83705BDA:r)
---
|
### Rand Paul's Sneak Attack Strategy
Rand Paul was 2,487 miles away from the Beltway journalists who had written off his campaign, and he was loving it. Paul's five-day run was taking him through Western states far from the key battlefields of 2016, drawing him out on issues that don't resonate in Iowa and New Hampshire. That played to Paul's strengths.

[ ](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/30058151:28809734773:m:1:879035888:1B532A49DEC55C01466E3B2B83705BDA:r)
---
|
### Op-Ed: Real Conservatives Stand for Private Property
As conservatives, we seek to bind government using the rule of law to prevent the abuse of power. I ran for office in 2010 after spending my life outside of both big business and politics. I ran as a member of the Tea Party who was sick and tired of it all. Of the politicians. Of the people who sought to buy the politicians. Of the entire Washington machine.

[ ](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/30058153:28809734773:m:1:879035888:1B532A49DEC55C01466E3B2B83705BDA:r)

name: Katie McGinty
subject: Our first month
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
body:
---
Friend,

| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7783507585428094976/-2831899949476610048?refcode=20150829e&amount=15)
---
The past few weeks have been absolutely incredible. Since announcing my campaign for Senate, folks across Pennsylvania and around the country have stepped up in a big way. I can't thank you enough.

We've got a tough fight ahead. Pennsylvania is a must-win state in 2016, and Pat Toomey and his special interest friends are going to fight hard.

**The most important thing we can do right now is finish our first month strong and demonstrate the unprecedented grassroots support behind our campaign.**

[**I 've set an ambitious first month fundraising goal for August, and I'm hoping I can count on you to help us get there. Can you pitch in $5 right away?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7783507585428094976/-2831899949476610048?refcode=20150829e&amount=15)

We know Pat Toomey is out-of-touch with Pennsylvania.

As a Senator, he's voted to take affordable health care coverage away from millions of Americans, defund Planned Parenthood, kill good paying manufacturing jobs by supporting unfair trade deals, and dramatically slash investments that protect our air, water, and clean energy resources. **But it 's going to take all of us working together to build the strong, grassroots-powered campaign necessary to defeat Pat Toomey and end his partisan-driven agenda in the Senate.**

[**We need to raise another $5,255 before Monday to stay on track towards our month-end goal. Can I count on you to chip in $5?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7783507585428094976/-2831899949476610048?refcode=20150829e&amount=15)

Thanks for all of your support,

Katie

body:
Dear Fellow Conservative,

Who has the momentum going into September?

That's the question that will be on every
reporter's mind as this month winds down to an end.

Fellow Conservative, don't be fooled. Our national media
loves to pump up phony conservative candidates only to tear them
down a few weeks later. And I believe this race for the
Republican nomination for President has many more shake ups along
the way.

But this end of month Fundraising Blitz is
absolutely critical, and I'm counting on you to help me take the
"next step."

Will you please agree to your most generous
contribution of $20.16 today?

I believe the American people want a true
limited government champion.

Unlike most politicians, I delivered on the
Tea Party message I ran on in 2010.

Now, I'm running for President because I
believe we need someone who is serious about turning this country
around -- not another Big Government candidate from EITHER party.

Whether it's stopping reauthorization of some
of the worst provisions of the so-called "PATRIOT Act," forcing
Congress to vote on my bill to defund Planned Parenthood, or
issuing a jaw-dropping new tax plan to implement a 14.5% Fair and
Flat tax, you and I are turning heads all over the country.

But now, it's time to take the next step.

With August coming to an end, your support
has never been more critical.

So please agree to your most generous
contribution of $20.16 or more today!

In Liberty,

Rand Paul

| | Friend, I am running for President to restore moral clarity to our country and reverse course on the long laundry list of failures of Barack Obama’s presidency: Obamacare, amnesty, sanctuary cities, out of control spending and a general disregard for our founding principles. I cannot watch anymore as our culture and our principles erode under Barack Obama, Hillary Clinton and the left wing liberal elites clustered in Washington, our media and in New York. The America we live in today would be hardly recognizable to our Founding Fathers. We’ve abandoned our founding principles for the failed governance and failed ideas that permeate this Washington to Wall Street axis of power. To help Americans understand what we have lost, earlier this year I published a book called [God, Guns, Grits and Gravy](http://clk.mikehuckabee.com/?r=MTAwMQ0KSjY1NDAtN2JjNDc3ZTMtYjk2MC00MjA2LTg0NWYtYzlkMzFhMjhhZGM4DQozNTVkYTQwMC04NTE0LTRkNDYtYWNkMi1hZDMxYTRmMWIyOGUNCjc2MDAwMDA2OWRmMmNiDQpodHRwczovL3d3dy5taWtlaHVja2FiZWUuY29tL2RvbmF0ZTEwP3M9MzE1Uw0KdHJ1ZQ0KZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20%3d). It seems that’s all the Washington establishment is capable of these days. Last year we were promised if we gave control of the United States Senate to Republicans they would pass a bill to repeal ObamaCare, nearly ten months later, we’re still waiting. [**If you think it’s time for the Washington crowd to put up or shut up make an immediate contribution of $25, $75, $100, $250, $500 or any amount you can afford today.**](http://links.connectivistmedia.mkt7175.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjMzODQ5ODUS1&r=MTIyNzQ5MzU4NDU1S0&b=0&j=NjIyODQ2MjE1S0&mt=1&rt=0) I ran for Governor of Wisconsin because I knew our state was in trouble. Once elected we took big, bold actions and balanced the budget, cut the unemployment rate to 4.6%, and beat the public sector union bosses. Taking on the status quo was not easy, we faced death threats, nearly 100,000 protestors, and ultimately a recall election. We took all those fights head-on and won because we stood true to our conservative principles. If we can do all this in a blue state like Wisconsin, you can be sure we can accomplish the same successes in Washington. Our next president must be a leader unafraid to take on the liberal special interests or anyone else who would stand in the way of conservative change. name: Yvette Lewis
subject: From the Front Lines: A Campaign Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Dear Friend, As we head into the waning days of summer, our campaign continues to charge ahead, full steam and gaining momentum with each passing day. It's a reflection of the priorities that Chris Van Hollen has always had -- and not just during his U.S. Senate candidacy, but over his entire career as an effective leader who gets things done for Maryland families. Labor Day is almost upon us, and for all the volunteers, interns and staff working tirelessly at Van Hollen HQ, there's nothing more exciting than seeing the swell of incredible grassroots energy as Chris meets with voters in communities all across Maryland. We know this race is just starting to heat up, but there's no denying the potency of Chris' message of progressive leadership, effectiveness and getting things done for hardworking families in this state. After all, that's Chris Van Hollen's record. **COMMUNITY OUTREACH AND EVENTS:** With Congress in August recess, it has been an incredibly busy month for this campaign -- and nobody is working harder than Chris. We've held nearly a dozen meet-and-greet events with huge turnouts in every corner of the state. These are a chance for anyone to come out and hear from Chris about his vision for Maryland, or just to share their thoughts on issues with our next U.S. Senator. Katherine Van Hollen has been doing similar meetings with women activists who are eager to get involved with our campaign. **ENDORSEMENTS:** Chris continues to rack up support from key community advocates, local elected officials and statewide progressive leaders who know his record of getting things done for Marylanders and have thrown their support behind his candidacy. We're talking about individuals who have already earned the trust of voters in their towns, neighborhoods and cities across Maryland. And that's exactly why it matters when Delegate Maggie McIntosh stands alongside Chris at a Baltimore diner and he receives support from respected Baltimore leaders; or when former Delegate and progressive champion Heather Mizeur urges her supporters to mobilize for Chris; or when a powerhouse State Senator like Joanne Benson urges her Prince Georges' County constituents to support Chris because of his record of delivering for students seniors, women and working families. Outside observers may sometimes miss this significance of endorsements like these, as well as so many others that Chris has already secured. But plugged-in Democrats like you understand why having trusted community allies gives Chris an important edge. Other key endorsements recently include Sen. Jim Mathias of Ocean City, an influential and beloved Democrat on the Eastern Shore, and Del. Erek Barron of Prince George's County, who just last week became the latest of a telling number of elected officials from Maryland's 4th congressional district to get behind Chris in this election. **ENDORSEMENTS HEADLINES:** **[Mizeur backs Van Hollen for Senate](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b13/89139705/VEsH/ "http://www.baltimoresun.com/news/maryland/politics/blog/bal-mizeur-backs-van-hollen-for-senate-20150826-story.html")** By John Fritze, The Baltimore Sun, August 26, 2015 "Heather R. Mizeur, a former state lawmaker who developed an energetic following among Maryland progressives during her run for governor last year, is endorsing Rep. Chris Van Hollen for Senate, according to a video **[posted on her Facebook page](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b10/89139705/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/VanHollenForMD/posts/10153121647836732")** Wednesday." [**Van Hollen picks up Baltimore endorsements**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b11/89139705/VEsF/ "http://www.baltimoresun.com/news/maryland/politics/blog/bal-van-hollen-picks-up-baltimore-endorsements-20150821-story.html") By John Fritze, The Baltimore Sun, August 21, 2015 "City Councilwoman Rochelle "Rikki" Spector, former Maryland Attorney General Stephen H. Sachs and longtime Democratic power broker Rick O. Berndt are supporting Van Hollen's effort to replace retiring Sen. Barbara A. Mikulski" [**Influential Baltimore politician backs Chris Van Hollen for Senate**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b2e/89139705/VEsC/ "http://www.washingtonpost.com/news/post-politics/wp/2015/07/30/influential-baltimore-politician-backs-chris-van-hollen-for-senate/") By Rachel Weiner, The Washington Post, July 30, 2015 "Rep. Chris Van Hollen scored a major endorsement Thursday in the Maryland Democratic Senate primary, winning the backing of state Del. Maggie McIntosh (D)." **ALSO IN THE NEWS:** You may have heard this week that Sen. Ted Cruz is once again threatening to shut down the federal government -- this time over funding for Planned Parenthood. Well it should come as no surprise that Chris is fighting back, just like he did when Cruz and his Tea Party allies tried to bring us to a standstill in 2013 to protest funding for the Affordable Care Act. Check out Chris' interview on MSNBC Wednesday night where he discussed the looming shutdown crisis and the need for effective leadership to confront the Tea Party's irresponsible attack on the rights and health care of women across this country. [](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b2f/89139705/VEsD/ "https://www.facebook.com/VanHollenForMD/videos/10153123499571732/") [**WATCH CHRIS ON MSNBC **](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b2f/89139705/VEsA/ "https://www.facebook.com/VanHollenForMD/videos/10153123499571732/") **LEGISLATIVE UPDATE:** While Chris is working hard to connect with voters across Maryland, he remains focused on the issues at hand as a national leader for commonsense progressive solutions to address our biggest challenges. Here's just a few of the bills he's working on:
* As he's done throughout his career, Chris is taking on the NRA to advance meaningful gun safety legislation -- just like he did in the Maryland Senate when he passed Maryland's historic law to require built-in trigger locks on handgun sales. Chris fought the powerful gun lobby -- and even members of his own party -- to get that law passed, and now once again he's taking up that fight. Chris' new legislation would create incentivizes for other states to replicate what Maryland did nearly two years ago: pass a law that requires anyone purchasing a handgun to first obtain a license, the same way we require licenses to drive a car or fish in a river. The Washington Post recently called Chris' bill, "**[a sensible, state-based answer to gun violence.](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b2c/89139705/VEsB/ "https://www.washingtonpost.com/opinions/a-sensible-state-based-answer-to-gun-violence/2015/07/28/f779dc0c-113a-11e5-9726-49d6fa26a8c6_story.html")** "

* Our nation's infrastructure is outdated and desperately in need of maintenance and repair, and to make matters worse the Highway Trust Fund is drying up. Chris is sponsoring a bill that would close an international tax loophole that allows corporations to dodge their responsibilities to American taxpayers, and instead use that revenue -- $40 billion -- to invest in modernizing our nation's vital infrastructure.

As the former chair of the Maryland Democratic Party, and now as co-chair of Chris's campaign, I couldn't be more excited by the genuine grassroots people-powered momentum we're seeing on the ground, and that's all thanks to incredible supporters like you. It's your energy, generosity and hard work that makes the difference every single day, and I hope you know just how grateful Chris is for everything that you do. Yvette Lewis Co-Chair, Van Hollen for U.S. Senate P.S. Don't miss out on future updates from Chris and the rest of the team. Be sure to follow us on Facebook and Twitter. | [](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b2d/89139705/VEsO/ "https://www.facebook.com/VanHollenForMD") | [](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91f2a/70cdc2ba/47a32609/3955b2a/89139705/VEsP/ "https://twitter.com/vanhollenformd")

clean_body: Dear Friend, As we head into the waning days of summer, our campaign continues to charge ahead, full steam and gaining momentum with each passing day. It's a reflection of the priorities that Chris Van Hollen has always had -- and not just during his U.S. Senate candidacy, but over his entire career as an effective leader who gets things done for Maryland families. Labor Day is almost upon us, and for all the volunteers, interns and staff working tirelessly at Van Hollen HQ, there's nothing more exciting than seeing the swell of incredible grassroots energy as Chris meets with voters in communities all across Maryland. We know this race is just starting to heat up, but there's no denying the potency of Chris' message of progressive leadership, effectiveness and getting things done for hardworking families in this state. After all, that's Chris Van Hollen's record. COMMUNITY OUTREACH AND EVENTS: With Congress in August recess, it has been an incredibly busy month for this campaign -- and nobody is working harder than Chris. We've held nearly a dozen meet-and-greet events with huge turnouts in every corner of the state. These are a chance for anyone to come out and hear from Chris about his vision for Maryland, or just to share their thoughts on issues with our next U.S. Senator. Katherine Van Hollen has been doing similar meetings with women activists who are eager to get involved with our campaign. ENDORSEMENTS: Chris continues to rack up support from key community advocates, local elected officials and statewide progressive leaders who know his record of getting things done for Marylanders and have thrown their support behind his candidacy. We're talking about individuals who have already earned the trust of voters in their towns, neighborhoods and cities across Maryland. And that's exactly why it matters when Delegate Maggie McIntosh stands alongside Chris at a Baltimore diner and he receives support from respected Baltimore leaders; or when former Delegate and progressive champion Heather Mizeur urges her supporters to mobilize for Chris; or when a powerhouse State Senator like Joanne Benson urges her Prince Georges' County constituents to support Chris because of his record of delivering for students seniors, women and working families. Outside observers may sometimes miss this significance of endorsements like these, as well as so many others that Chris has already secured. But plugged-in Democrats like you understand why having trusted community allies gives Chris an important edge. Other key endorsements recently include Sen. Jim Mathias of Ocean City, an influential and beloved Democrat on the Eastern Shore, and Del. Erek Barron of Prince George's County, who just last week became the latest of a telling number of elected officials from Maryland's 4th congressional district to get behind Chris in this election. ENDORSEMENTS HEADLINES: "" By John Fritze, The Baltimore Sun, August 26, 2015 "Heather R. Mizeur, a former state lawmaker who developed an energetic following among Maryland progressives during her run for governor last year, is endorsing Rep. Chris Van Hollen for Senate, according to a video "" Wednesday." "" By John Fritze, The Baltimore Sun, August 21, 2015 "City Councilwoman Rochelle "Rikki" Spector, former Maryland Attorney General Stephen H. Sachs and longtime Democratic power broker Rick O. Berndt are supporting Van Hollen's effort to replace retiring Sen. Barbara A. Mikulski" "" By Rachel Weiner, The Washington Post, July 30, 2015 "Rep. Chris Van Hollen scored a major endorsement Thursday in the Maryland Democratic Senate primary, winning the backing of state Del. Maggie McIntosh (D)." ALSO IN THE NEWS: You may have heard this week that Sen. Ted Cruz is once again threatening to shut down the federal government -- this time over funding for Planned Parenthood. Well it should come as no surprise that Chris is fighting back, just like he did when Cruz and his Tea Party allies tried to bring us to a standstill in 2013 to protest funding for the Affordable Care Act. Check out Chris' interview on MSNBC Wednesday night where he discussed the looming shutdown crisis and the need for effective leadership to confront the Tea Party's irresponsible attack on the rights and health care of women across this country. "" "" LEGISLATIVE UPDATE: While Chris is working hard to connect with voters across Maryland, he remains focused on the issues at hand as a national leader for commonsense progressive solutions to address our biggest challenges. Here's just a few of the bills he's working on: As he's done throughout his career, Chris is taking on the NRA to advance meaningful gun safety legislation -- just like he did in the Maryland Senate when he passed Maryland's historic law to require built-in trigger locks on handgun sales. Chris fought the powerful gun lobby -- and even members of his own party -- to get that law passed, and now once again he's taking up that fight. Chris' new legislation would create incentivizes for other states to replicate what Maryland did nearly two years ago: pass a law that requires anyone purchasing a handgun to first obtain a license, the same way we require licenses to drive a car or fish in a river. The Washington Post recently called Chris' bill, " "" " Our nation's infrastructure is outdated and desperately in need of maintenance and repair, and to make matters worse the Highway Trust Fund is drying up. Chris is sponsoring a bill that would close an international tax loophole that allows corporations to dodge their responsibilities to American taxpayers, and instead use that revenue -- $40 billion -- to invest in modernizing our nation's vital infrastructure. As the former chair of the Maryland Democratic Party, and now as co-chair of Chris's campaign, I couldn't be more excited by the genuine grassroots people-powered momentum we're seeing on the ground, and that's all thanks to incredible supporters like you. It's your energy, generosity and hard work that makes the difference every single day, and I hope you know just how grateful Chris is for everything that you do. Yvette Lewis Co-Chair, Van Hollen for U.S. Senate P.S. Don't miss out on future updates from Chris and the rest of the team.
